message:
🎨 fix: card +/kebab overlap, PredictiveHealth button align, EventStream pagination (#8383 #8385 #8381) (#8401)

* 🎨 fix: card +/kebab overlap, PredictiveHealth button align, EventStream pagination (#8383 #8385 #8381)

#8383: PR #8337 moved the hover "+" add-card button from the
overflowing `-top-2 -right-2` into the card at `top-2 right-2`, but
the kebab / maximize / report-issue action row lives at roughly
`right: 16px` inside the `px-4 py-3` card header — so on hover the
"+" sat directly on top of the three-dots menu. Anchor the "+" to the
right edge centered vertically (`top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 right-2`),
which still reads as "insert another card next to this one" but no
longer collides with header controls.

#8385: `CardSearchInput` wraps its <input> in a div with a baked-in
`mb-4`, so when ConsoleOfflineDetectionCard puts the search input
inline with the list/root-cause view-mode toggle, the search wrapper
is visually taller than the toggle group even though `items-center`
is set. The extra margin makes the toggle buttons look misaligned.
Pass `!mb-0` via className to flatten the wrapper for the inline
usage — standalone CardSearchInput callers are unaffected.

#8381: In `useCardData`, `goToPage` read `totalPages` directly from
its render closure. When a background refresh (realtime-category
events poll) expanded the dataset between the Next-click and the
render that observed the new totalPages, goToPage would clamp
against the *pre-refresh* total (often 1) — silently snapping the
user back to page 1. Stash totalPages in a ref (synced via an
effect) so goToPage always clamps against the latest value. Also
switch the totalPages-shrank guard effect to functional setState
so it reads the current `currentPage` without needing it in deps
(#5762 closure trap). Adds a deep test that simulates the grow-
then-advance scenario.

Fixes #8383
Fixes #8385
Fixes #8381

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 fix: add * continuation to JSX comment so #8385 issue-ref doesn't trip hex ratchet

The multi-line JSX comment added in this PR referenced #8385 but its
continuation lines started with '#8385:' and normal text rather than
'* ', so shouldSkipLine() only skipped the first line ({/*) and the
raw-hex detector matched '#8385' as a 4-digit hex color. That pushed
the ratchet count from 265 to 266.

Prefix each continuation line with ' * ' so the skip rule
(stripped.startsWith('*')) covers the whole comment block.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -148,7 +148,14 @@ export const SortableCard = memo(function SortableCard({ card, onConfigure, onRe
       {onInsertAfter && (
         <button
           onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onInsertAfter() }}
-          className="absolute top-2 right-2 z-20 opacity-0 group-hover/card:opacity-100 transition-all w-6 h-6 rounded-full bg-primary text-primary-foreground flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-bold shadow-lg hover:scale-110 ring-2 ring-background"
+          // #8383: Anchor to the right edge centered vertically so the "+"
+          // sits between this card and the next column, not on top of the
+          // card header action row where the kebab / maximize / report
+          // buttons live. `top-2 right-2` from the original #8337 fix put
+          // this button in the same coordinate space as the kebab menu
+          // (which sits at roughly right:16px inside the header) causing
+          // a direct overlap at rest and on hover.
+          className="absolute top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 right-2 z-20 opacity-0 group-hover/card:opacity-100 transition-all w-6 h-6 rounded-full bg-primary text-primary-foreground flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-bold shadow-lg hover:scale-110 ring-2 ring-background"
           aria-label="Add card"
           title="Add card here"
         >

@@ -299,6 +299,28 @@ describe('useCardData deep branches', () => {
     // Sort no longer resets page — user stays on current page (#5209)
     expect(result.current.currentPage).toBe(3)
   })
+
+  it('goToPage reads totalPages from a ref so a Next click after data grows still advances (#8381)', () => {
+    // Simulate the EventStream scenario: the card mounts with a small
+    // initial dataset (1 page), then a background refresh expands the
+    // dataset to multiple pages. Before the ref fix, `goToPage` would
+    // clamp against the *initial* totalPages=1 captured in its render
+    // closure, so clicking Next silently snapped back to page 1.
+    const fewItems = items.slice(0, 3) // 3 items / page 5 = 1 page
+    const { result, rerender } = renderHook(
+      ({ data }: { data: TestItem[] }) => useCardData(data, config),
+      { initialProps: { data: fewItems } },
+    )
+    expect(result.current.totalPages).toBe(1)
+
+    // Data grows (simulates background refresh arriving).
+    rerender({ data: items })
+    expect(result.current.totalPages).toBe(3)
+
+    // Clicking Next must honor the *latest* totalPages, not the stale one.
+    act(() => { result.current.goToPage(2) })
+    expect(result.current.currentPage).toBe(2)
+  })
 })
 
 // ============================================================================
